jokes.text: I am from Hamburg, ja!
jokes.setup: Character announces plan to pretend to be foreign in Times Square
jokes.punchline: Lutz's terrible German accent attempt
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Lutz's earnest incompetence, economical delivery

jokes.text: Hey, Liz Lemon, I'm going to an animals-only strip club. Interested?
jokes.setup: Character casually invites Liz to join various activities
jokes.punchline: Escalates to completely absurd strip club invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation, highly quotable, great economy of language

jokes.text: Does that mean the animals strip or the animals are the customers? / Animal customers? That's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Liz asks logical clarification about absurd strip club
jokes.punchline: Creed dismisses the less ridiculous interpretation as ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic inversion - dismissing the logical option as ridiculous while accepting the absurd premise

jokes.text: Big night, Lemon? Let me guess. Meatball sub, extra bread, bottle of NyQuil, TiVo 'Top Chef,' a little Miss Bonnie Raitt, lights out.
jokes.setup: Jack notices Liz picking up food order
jokes.punchline: Detailed prediction of her pathetic evening routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Jack's knowledge of Liz's sad routines, specific details make it funny

jokes.text: Then you won't mind when I tell you that Casey gets voted off tonight. / You monster! Why are you like this?
jokes.setup: Jack doesn't believe Liz has real plans
jokes.punchline: Jack cruelly spoils her show, Liz's outraged reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics, Jack's cruelty and Liz's melodramatic response work well together

jokes.text: Dijonaise is a boy's name? Pardon me.
jokes.setup: Tracy introduces team including 'one special lady'
jokes.punchline: Jack assumes Dijonaise (condiment name) is male
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay on ridiculous name, Jack's polite confusion adds character element

jokes.text: I wasn't. My motorcycle hit a police horse. This is community service.
jokes.setup: Jack praises Tracy's interest in baseball
jokes.punchline: Tracy reveals he's only there due to bizarre accident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdism - specific, unexpected, delivered deadpan. Great economy.

jokes.text: These kids come from Knuckle Beach, the worst neighborhood in New York. They are poor as hell.
jokes.setup: Tracy describes the kids' background
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous neighborhood name 'Knuckle Beach'
jokes.explanation: Absurd neighborhood name works as 30 Rock world-building, moderately funny

jokes.text: Everyone told us we were going to lose because our team was all white and the other team was completely... Uh... Anyway, we won
jokes.setup: Jack starts inspirational baseball story
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's about to say something inappropriate, awkwardly stops
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jack's obliviousness and last-second awareness

jokes.text: When I grow up, I'm going to do vending-machine maintenance. / I'm going to get shot by a cop and sue the city. / I'm going to be a talkative doorman with a drinking problem.
jokes.setup: Jack asks kids about their dreams
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific, depressing career aspirations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of inspirational moment with darkly specific, realistic 'dreams' - highly quotable

jokes.text: 0-17. / Damn! We supposed to be at the game right now. / 0-18! / That one's on Coach Tracy.
jokes.setup: Tracy learning the team's record
jokes.punchline: Immediate addition to their losing streak while Tracy processes the information
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke with perfect timing, shows Tracy's coaching incompetence

jokes.text: That's right. You shoot for the stars.
jokes.setup: Kids share their depressing life goals
jokes.punchline: Jack's oblivious encouragement of terrible aspirations
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing Jack's complete disconnection from reality of their situation

jokes.text: Someday I'll have an office like this. To clean.
jokes.setup: Kid looks around Jack's impressive office admiringly
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only aspires to clean it, not own it
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy - setup creates expectation, punchline deflates it perfectly

jokes.text: Like R. Kelly and Michael Vick.
jokes.setup: Jack suggests they need role models
jokes.punchline: Kid suggests two highly problematic celebrities
jokes.explanation: Dark humor based on real controversies, somewhat cheap but delivered well

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Who ordered the veal? Am I right, guys?
jokes.setup: Jenna sees young, attractive guy
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate comment about his youth
jokes.explanation: Predictable cougar joke but fits Jenna's character, somewhat obvious

jokes.text: I like your blond streaks. It's very Simon Le Bon. / Who? / Oh, you're young.
jokes.setup: Jenna tries to compliment young guy with 80s reference
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know who she's talking about, highlighting age gap
jokes.explanation: Good setup for age gap theme, works for character development

jokes.text: Give it up, Jenna. You're talking to an ultrasound.
jokes.setup: Jenna flirting with very young guy
jokes.punchline: Liz's devastating comparison to ultrasound image
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, original metaphor with perfect economy - highly quotable and visually funny

jokes.text: Hah, Frank's gay! / Maybe I am gay. For that little peach.
jokes.setup: Frank shows attraction to young guy
jokes.punchline: Frank's earnest consideration of being 'gay for one person'
jokes.explanation: Sets up Frank's subplot well, 'little peach' is endearing and funny

jokes.text: There's a weird dude standing over there, and I don't want to get shot today.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to end baseball practice
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to violence as reason to leave
jokes.explanation: Dark humor fitting Tracy's character and setting, moderately effective

jokes.text: That's why I sponsor a charity that gives away tuxedoes to homeless people. It was a beautiful ceremony. Mazel tov.
jokes.setup: Jack explains his 'dress for success' philosophy
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd charity giving formal wear to homeless people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack absurdism - out-of-touch wealthy perspective that's hilariously inappropriate

jokes.text: At our first practice, they asked me what the sun was.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains kids don't care about winning, just want to go outside
jokes.punchline: Extreme exaggeration of their indoor confinement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd exaggeration with social commentary undertones, great economy

jokes.text: Did you just come from working out, or are you naturally veiny?
jokes.setup: Frank trying to flirt with Jamie
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and awkward pickup line
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Frank's complete lack of game, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: Just gellin' like Magellan.
jokes.setup: Frank asks what Jamie's doing Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Jamie uses dated slang expression
jokes.explanation: Mild joke playing on dated expression, more character development than comedy

jokes.text: Jamie, how old do you think I am? / I don't know. 29? / Wow. You are good at guessing ages. I am exactly 29.
jokes.setup: Frank fishing for age compliment
jokes.punchline: Jamie's guess is hilariously off, Frank takes it as validation
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Frank's self-deception and desperation

jokes.text: I bought you a sweater. It's slim-fitting. They call it a French cut. It wasn't on sale or anything.
jokes.setup: Frank continuing to pursue Jamie awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed gift explanation showing his desperation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing Frank's over-eagerness, specific details make it funnier

jokes.text: You can't be gay for just one person unless you're a lady and you meet Ellen.
jokes.setup: Frank claims to be 'gay for Jamie'
jokes.punchline: Toofer's exception for Ellen DeGeneres
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that feels earned, good structure and economy

jokes.text: I had my 'no sex with Asians' rule. But then one day you walk into Sharper Image, and there's Quan.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to relate to Liz's age-lying situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate racial 'rule' and specific encounter
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Jenna moment, somewhat problematic but fits character

jokes.text: Cougars. Hot older ladies pouncing on their young prey.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining the cougar phenomenon to Liz
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic description with predatory metaphor
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode theme, Jenna's dramatic delivery helps but concept is familiar

jokes.text: The one with the 'Crisis in Africa' cover. God, it makes me so sad that more people don't know about cougars.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions Vanity Fair article about cougars
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposes serious global crisis with trivial dating trend
jokes.explanation: Excellent character beat showing Jenna's self-absorption and twisted priorities

jokes.text: You should get a Ring Pop to suck on.
jokes.setup: Jenna giving Liz cougar advice about acting young
jokes.punchline: Suggests infantile candy as age-reduction strategy
jokes.explanation: Absurd but specific advice that visualizes well, good economy

jokes.text: A world where orange soda is an acceptable substitute for breast milk!
jokes.setup: Tracy describing how different Knuckle Beach is
jokes.punchline: Extreme, disturbing example of neighborhood's problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd exaggeration that's both funny and darkly social commentary

jokes.text: It's like this great country of ours. We can go into any nation, impose our values, and make things better. It's what Bush is doing all over the globe.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his approach to helping the kids
jokes.punchline: Oblivious endorsement of controversial foreign policy
jokes.explanation: Good political satire through Jack's character, shows his worldview perfectly

jokes.text: Bush? Now, I don't want to go off on a rant here.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Bush
jokes.punchline: Tracy references Dennis Miller catchphrase instead of ranting
jokes.explanation: Good meta reference that deflates expected political rant

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. This is my dream journal. They've all come true so far.
jokes.setup: Kenneth shows wrong drawing to Jack
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has prophetic dreams that come true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - casually reveals impossible supernatural ability in his innocent way

jokes.text: You can't wear blue in Knuckle Beach, which is hard because jeans go with everything.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining missing uniforms
jokes.punchline: Gang color restrictions create fashion dilemma
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of serious gang violence with mundane fashion concerns

jokes.text: Speaking of music I like, how about Gnarls Barkley, huh? That guy's great. Have you been to his official website?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to seem young and hip on her date
jokes.punchline: Shows she doesn't know Gnarls Barkley is a duo, not a person
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's desperate attempt to seem current

jokes.text: I'm 37! / I'm 20. / This just went from a senior dating a freshman to Mary Kay Letourneau and Vili Fualaau.
jokes.setup: Age reveal creates awkward moment
jokes.punchline: Liz compares their relationship to infamous teacher-student scandal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with topical reference, shows Liz's tendency toward melodrama

jokes.text: Are those friends of yours? / Oh, when will death come?
jokes.setup: Jamie doesn't recognize the scandal reference
jokes.punchline: Liz's dramatic despair at the generation gap
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing the complete cultural disconnect between them

jokes.text: Where did you two meet? An Amber Alert?
jokes.setup: Jack sees Liz with much younger date
jokes.punchline: Suggests their age gap is criminal
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - sharp, unexpected, and quotable

jokes.text: Boy, the art in here is hung really level.
jokes.setup: Jack asks if Liz looks ridiculous with younger men
jokes.punchline: Liz avoids answering by commenting on gallery art
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward deflection showing Liz's diplomatic evasion skills

jokes.text: You've got money, status, naturally thick hair, a decent set.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Liz is a catch
jokes.punchline: List ends with crude physical assessment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jack's inappropriate but well-meaning advice style

jokes.text: I went up on my roof the other day.
jokes.setup: Jack asks why Liz is against having fun
jokes.punchline: Liz's pathetic example of 'fun' activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment showing her low bar for excitement, great character integration

jokes.text: And they all seem to really hate my grandpa 'cause they keep yelling, 'Kill Whitey.' And I'm like, 'What do you think you are, alcohol?'
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining team problems to Jack
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent misunderstanding of racial tensions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth moment - his innocent confusion about race creates absurd interpretation

jokes.text: 'Betrayal'... colon... 'What Really Happened With My Baseball Team'... comma... 'Disaster at Knuckle Beach'... question mark.
jokes.setup: Tracy threatens to write tell-all book
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed punctuation specifications for dramatic title
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - detailed punctuation specs make the threat comically specific

jokes.text: And they won't listen, especially when I say, 'Don't hit me with my own shoes.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing the team's chaos
jokes.punchline: Specific, pathetic detail about his victimization
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth victimization moment, specific detail makes it funnier than generic violence

jokes.text: Guess you're not the only cougar in town. Aidan's a freshman at NYU. / Sure. / Give me change for a soda? / You just had one. / Whatever. / Hey! What did I say about you wearing your Heelys inside?
jokes.setup: Cerie showing off her young boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's essentially a child through series of exchanges
jokes.explanation: Great escalating reveal - each exchange makes Aidan seem younger and more childish

jokes.text: Is it like that time they found my grandpa at the bus station?
jokes.setup: Cerie asking about Liz being at a club
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to finding elderly relative in concerning situation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits Cerie's ditzy but occasionally insightful character

jokes.text: Hey! What did I say about you wearing your Heelys inside?
jokes.setup: Cerie with her young 'boyfriend' Aidan
jokes.punchline: She scolds him like a parent about children's shoes
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag showing how young Aidan really is, good character moment for Cerie

jokes.text: His name is Jamie, and you know what? I don't spend a lot of time worrying about his feelings.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her relationship
jokes.punchline: Claims emotional detachment while clearly being emotional about it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's attempt to act casual while clearly invested

jokes.text: That's great, Maude. How's Harold?
jokes.setup: Liz boasting about her boy toy
jokes.punchline: Frank's sarcastic response using old-person names
jokes.explanation: Good put-down showing Frank's dry wit, old-fashioned names work well

jokes.text: I made you a painting. It's called 'One-Armed Mermaid That's Part Unicorn With Bigfoot.'
jokes.setup: Jamie gives Liz a gift
jokes.punchline: Comically elaborate and nonsensical art piece title
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific - captures youthful artistic pretension perfectly

jokes.text: Which ReShon?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions letting ReShon contact birth mother
jokes.punchline: Implies there are multiple kids named ReShon
jokes.explanation: Great economy - two words that imply the chaos of multiple kids with same name

jokes.text: One word. Surge. / That's two words!
jokes.setup: Jack announces his plan with dramatic buildup
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately calls out the word count error
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Jack's dramatic moment, good character dynamic

jokes.text: Tracy, I'll pay somebody to read them for you.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking if he can throw away the kids' letters without reading them
jokes.punchline: Jack's solution that completely misses the emotional point
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment showing his wealth-based solutions to emotional problems

jokes.text: We're just two straight guys who want to enjoy each other's bodies.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to convince Jamie they're both not gay
jokes.punchline: Describes extremely gay scenario while claiming straightness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - completely contradictory reasoning delivered with total sincerity

jokes.text: These Dominican birth certificates say otherwise.
jokes.setup: Opposition questions ages of players
jokes.punchline: Jack casually reveals they're using fake documents
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Jack's win-at-all-costs mentality, delivered casually

jokes.text: Really? I've never been in a cab with less than seven people.
jokes.setup: Liz suggests taking a cab
jokes.punchline: Jamie reveals his inexperience with basic luxury
jokes.explanation: Good class/economic humor showing the gap between Liz and Jamie's experiences

jokes.text: All right, Mom. I'm taking off! / Mom? / Yeah, I'm 20. I can't afford this place.
jokes.setup: Jamie calls out to someone as they leave
jokes.punchline: Reveals he still lives with his mother
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and cringe moment highlighting the age/maturity gap

jokes.text: Yep, that's what we look like. Shut it down.
jokes.setup: Liz sees herself with Jamie's mother
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition of how inappropriate the situation appears
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of self-awareness and decisive action, great economy

jokes.text: Sexual incompatibility, social faux pas, meeting their parents.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how May-December relationships end
jokes.punchline: List escalating to the most humiliating scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the parents meeting being the final humiliation, feels true

jokes.text: A younger companion makes you feel more alive. Opens you up to new experiences, fresh points of view, stimulating conversation. / Stop repeating what I say.
jokes.setup: Jack giving advice about younger partners
jokes.punchline: Liz points out he's just repeating her earlier words
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jack's tendency to recycle wisdom, meta element works well

jokes.text: What? No. I mean this company is my girlfriend. She gives me all the loving I'll ever want or need. / That's gross, Jack.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if Jack is seeing someone
jokes.punchline: Jack's creepy metaphor about his relationship with his company
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's workaholic tendencies taken to creepy extreme

jokes.text: What? No. I mean this company is my girlfriend. She gives me all the loving I'll ever want or need. / That's gross, Jack.
jokes.setup: Jack deflecting question about dating
jokes.punchline: Jack's creepy corporate relationship metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's work obsession and Liz's appropriate disgust

jokes.text: I'm not gay-gay. I'm just gay for Jamie.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to explain his attraction to other gay men
jokes.punchline: Insists on distinction between general and specific gayness
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier theme, Frank's earnest distinction is endearingly absurd

jokes.text: I'm not gay-gay. I'm just gay for Jamie.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to explain his sexuality to other gay men
jokes.punchline: Frank's confused distinction that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's sexual confusion, his logic still doesn't make sense

jokes.text: Bye, sexy bear!
jokes.setup: Frank leaving the gay club
jokes.punchline: Flamboyant goodbye from club patron
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny character bit, somewhat stereotypical but brief

jokes.text: Bye, sexy bear!
jokes.setup: Frank leaving the gay club
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top gay stereotype farewell
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotype humor, though it serves the story of Frank's realization

jokes.text: My muffin top is all that? Whole grain, low fat? I know you want a piece of that? But I just want to dance?
jokes.setup: Liz performing in music video
jokes.punchline: Song about her unflattering body part with food puns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant musical comedy moment - self-deprecating but empowering, highly memorable and quotable
